intro: Ourisia simpsonii, also known as New Zealand forget-me-not, is a flowering plant species endemic to New Zealand, characterized by its delicate blue flowers and creeping habit. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ourisia simpsonii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Ourisia simpsonii is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as in topical creams and ointments for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties.
